agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through states: trial, active, past-due, cancelled."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect recurring charges. Has a status indicating whether it can successfully process charges."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring time period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when a subscription charge is due."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated attempt to charge the customer's payment method to extend the subscription for another billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to process a renewal payment. Up to 3 attempts are made before escalation."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A time window after payment failure during which the subscription remains active before cancellation. 14 days for Premium tier."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A zero-cost subscription period allowing a customer to evaluate the service before committing to paid billing."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives enhanced billing leniency, specifically a 14-day grace period instead of the standard 3-strike cancellation rule."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription with a known-bad payment method would create an immediately-invalid billing state — the system would have an 'active' subscription it can never collect on. This protects data consistency: an active subscription must always have a viable path to payment. No VP policy change should allow deliberately onboarding broken payment states.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a subscription activation is attempted AND the associated payment method has status 'failed', THEN the activation MUST be rejected and the subscription MUST NOT transition to 'active'."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy parameter chosen to balance retry window against premature charging. A VP could change this to 5 or 10 days based on payment processor SLAs or customer feedback without corrupting system state. The system remains consistent regardless of whether renewal fires 3 days or 14 days before cycle end.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When the current date is exactly 7 days before a subscription's billing cycle end date, THEN the system MUST initiate an automatic renewal charge attempt."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The threshold of 3 attempts is a business policy decision balancing customer retention against revenue leakage risk. Management could change this to 2 or 5 attempts without breaking system integrity. The important invariant is that *some* finite limit exists, but the specific number is policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a non-Premium subscription has accumulated exactly 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts, THEN the system MUST automatically transition the subscription status to 'cancelled'."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. The 'cancelled' state is terminal by design. Allowing reactivation of cancelled subscriptions would break audit trails, potentially resurrect stale pricing or terms, and create ambiguity about which billing cycle applies. The one-way state transition from cancelled ensures clean lifecycle accounting and prevents data corruption in billing history.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When any operation attempts to transition a subscription from 'cancelled' to 'active', THEN the operation MUST be rejected. The only path to active service after cancellation is creating a new subscription entity."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Both the 14-day duration and the decision to give Premium tier preferential treatment are business policy choices. The grace period length could be adjusted to 21 days or 7 days, and the tier eligibility could be extended to other tiers, all without breaking system integrity. This is a retention strategy encoded as policy, not a physical or state constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a Premium tier subscription has a failed renewal, THEN the system MUST NOT cancel the subscription until 14 calendar days have elapsed since the first failure, regardless of retry attempt count."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data consistency and anti-abuse constraint. Allowing multiple concurrent trials per email would corrupt the uniqueness guarantee the system relies on to prevent trial abuse. It is analogous to a unique constraint in a database — if violated, the system is in an inconsistent state where a single customer is consuming multiple free-tier resources simultaneously. Unlike a policy threshold that can be tuned, the one-active-trial-per-identity rule is a fundamental integrity boundary.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a free trial subscription creation is attempted for an email address that already has an active trial subscription, THEN the creation MUST be rejected and no new trial subscription entity MUST be persisted."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer attempts to activate a subscription","when":"The payment method on file has a 'failed' status","then":"The system rejects the activation and returns an error indicating the payment method must be updated"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription exists with a billing cycle end date","when":"The current date is exactly 7 days before the billing cycle end date","then":"The system automatically initiates a renewal charge attempt against the stored payment method"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A non-Premium subscription with 2 prior consecutive failed renewal attempts","when":"The 3rd renewal attempt also fails","then":"The system automatically transitions the subscription to 'cancelled' status and notifies the customer"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription is in 'cancelled' status","when":"Any attempt is made to reactivate it (via API, UI, or internal process)","then":"The system rejects the reactivation and requires creation of a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal","when":"The first renewal failure occurs","then":"The system starts a 14-day grace period timer, continues retry attempts, and only cancels if payment is still unresolved after 14 days"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer with an active free trial subscription","when":"The same email address attempts to create another free trial","then":"The system rejects the second trial creation and returns an error indicating an active trial already exists"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A Premium tier subscription in its 14-day grace period","when":"A renewal payment succeeds on day 10 of the grace period","then":"The grace period is cleared, the subscription remains active, and the billing cycle resets normally"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A non-Premium subscription that has been auto-cancelled after 3 failures","when":"The customer wants to resume service","then":"The system requires creation of a new subscription entity; no 'resume' or 'reactivate' path exists"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails validation between renewal trigger (7 days before) and actual charge","expected_behavior":"The renewal attempt should fail and count toward the 3-strike or grace period logic, not silently skip"},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's payment method is updated during the 14-day grace period","expected_behavior":"System should immediately attempt renewal with the new payment method; if successful, grace period clears and subscription continues"},{"scenario":"Customer cancels and re-signs up with the same email within minutes","expected_behavior":"New subscription is created as a separate entity; old cancelled subscription remains unchanged in history"},{"scenario":"Free trial expires naturally (not cancelled) and customer tries another trial","expected_behavior":"If the first trial is no longer 'active' (expired/converted), a new trial should be permitted since the constraint is on concurrent active trials"},{"scenario":"Renewal triggers 7 days before cycle end, but all 3 retries fail before the cycle actually ends","expected_behavior":"Subscription is cancelled even though the paid period hasn't technically ended; the 3-strike rule takes precedence"},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber has payment fail, grace period starts, then they downgrade tier mid-grace-period","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — does the 14-day grace period continue or revert to 3-strike? Needs product decision"},{"scenario":"System clock issues cause renewal to trigger at 6 or 8 days before cycle end","expected_behavior":"System should have tolerance for minor timing variations but log deviations; the 7-day rule should be treated as approximate in distributed systems"},{"scenario":"Email address change on account with active free trial","expected_behavior":"Trial uniqueness should track the original email or account identity to prevent circumvention via email change"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct payment validation at activation prevents revenue leakage from subscriptions that can never collect. The 7-day renewal window maximizes successful payment collection by allowing retries. The 3-strike and grace period rules balance retention against carrying non-paying subscribers.","operational":"Terminal cancellation state simplifies subscription lifecycle management and audit trails. One-trial-per-email reduces abuse-driven support tickets and infrastructure costs from trial farming.","user":"Premium grace period creates tangible value differentiation for higher-paying customers, improving retention. Mandatory new subscription after cancellation ensures clean onboarding but may frustrate customers expecting a simple 'resume' button.","risk":"Trial abuse through email aliases (user+1@, user+2@) is a known circumvention vector not addressed by these rules. The cancelled-is-terminal rule may conflict with future win-back or pause features. Premium grace period without a retry count cap could theoretically allow indefinite retries within 14 days.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard tier)","impact":"3-strike rule means payment issues must be resolved quickly or service is lost permanently"},{"group":"Customers (Premium tier)","impact":"14-day grace period provides significantly more leniency, reinforcing premium value proposition"},{"group":"Finance/Revenue team","impact":"Auto-cancellation rules directly affect MRR churn metrics and revenue recognition timing"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"No reactivation path means support cannot override cancellation; they must guide customers through new subscription creation"},{"group":"Product/Growth team","impact":"One-trial-per-email limits growth experiment flexibility but protects unit economics"}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The spec is well-articulated with clear rules and explicit thresholds. No contradictions exist between rules. Minor ambiguities around Premium grace period interaction with the 3-strike rule and edge cases around tier changes exist but do not undermine the core rule set.","high_confidence_areas":["Payment method validation at activation is clearly an invariant","Cancelled-is-terminal state machine constraint","Free trial uniqueness per email","3-strike and 14-day grace period as policy parameters"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Premium grace period replaces or supplements the 3-strike retry mechanism — spec says 'instead of' but doesn't clarify max retries during grace period","How email uniqueness is enforced for trials — exact match vs normalized vs domain-aware","Whether the 7-day renewal window applies identically to all billing cycle lengths (weekly, monthly, annual)","Interaction between tier changes mid-grace-period"],"assumptions":["Premium grace period completely replaces the 3-strike rule, meaning retries continue throughout the 14 days without a hard attempt cap","Email matching for trial uniqueness is exact string match","The 7-day renewal lead time applies uniformly regardless of billing cycle length","Cancelled state is terminal with no future pause/hold feature planned"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking: Let me analyze these subscription billing rules carefully and classify each one.
